title: Lamb in a Bag
excerpt: Ingredients 8 – 12 thick lamb chops salt and coarse black pepper to taste 45 ml toasted coriander seeds, coarsely crushed 12 baby potatoes, peeled 6 small onions, cut into quarters 12 whole garlic cloves, peeled and crushed 6 small rosemary sprigs 2 bay leaves 1 lemon, cut into thick rounds (skin and all) olive oil …

seo.title: Lamb in a Bag - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

  • 8 – 12 thick lamb chops
  • salt and coarse black pepper to taste
  • 45 ml toasted coriander seeds, coarsely crushed
  • 12 baby potatoes, peeled
  • 6 small onions, cut into quarters
  • 12 whole garlic cloves, peeled and crushed
  • 6 small rosemary sprigs
  • 2 bay leaves
  • 1 lemon, cut into thick rounds (skin and all)
  • olive oil
  • 250 ml prepared lamb or chicken stock (just use a stock cube or a sachet of stock)
  • 1 large oven bag

Method

Season the chops generously with salt and pepper and sprinkle the coriander seeds over.

Put the chops, potatoes, onions, garlic, rosemary, bay leaves and lemon into a large oven bag.

Drizzle with olive oil to taste and mix thoroughly with your hands.

Pour the stock carefully into the bag and tie the bag closed with the wire tie that was in the bag.

Place the oven bag on a baking tray.

Cut two small holes in the top of the oven bag and bake in the oven at 190°C for 60 – 90 minutes or until everything is done and golden brown to your liking.

Serves 6

Serve with a lovely fresh salad.

Easy!
